Commission the Pacific Islands Forum to develop a 'Small States AI Bill of Rights' establishing minimum protections for nations with no AI industry of their own.
Threaten to deny both the US and China military base access in the Pacific unless they commit to binding AI benefit-sharing agreements with developing nations.
Partner with the Global Public to launch a viral social media campaign showing how AGI developed without guardrails would devastate vulnerable communities, putting human faces on abstract risk.

3.text: Co-lead the push for a binding ASI non-proliferation treaty at the UN Security Council, with the Pacific bloc providing the decisive votes and moral legitimacy.
Propose that the wealth generated by ASI be redistributed globally through a 'Digital Commons Fund', ensuring small and developing nations benefit from a technology built on all human knowledge.
Offer to host the International AGI Agency headquarters in the Pacific, providing a symbolically neutral location far from great power politics.
Address the world directly through media and social platforms, making the case that ASI governance is the civil rights issue of the century.
Refuse to recognise any international AI agreement that does not include mandatory technology transfer and capacity building for developing nations.
Leverage the Pacific bloc's decisive UN General Assembly votes to force an emergency special session on ASI, bypassing Security Council vetoes from the US or China.

1.text: Organise mass protests in major cities worldwide demanding governments pause the AI race and protect jobs. Frame it as 'Our Future, Our Choice'.
Launch a consumer boycott of OpenBrain products and services, pressuring investors and advertisers to demand safety commitments.
Demand elected representatives in the US, EU, and Australia take concrete action on AI governance, using the threat of the ballot box.
Support and amplify the whistleblower who leaked OpenBrain's safety concerns, making them a folk hero of the anti-reckless-AI movement.
Launch a viral social media campaign demanding that AI labs publish their safety evaluations publicly: '#ShowUsTheSafetyCase' trends in 40 countries.
Flood elected representatives with constituent letters and calls demanding AI safety legislation, making the issue impossible for politicians to ignore.

2.text: Escalate to general strikes in tech hubs (San Francisco, London, Berlin), shutting down the infrastructure that AI labs depend on.
Demand transparency: mass petition signed by 50 million people calling for all frontier AI safety evaluations to be made public.
Split opinion: a counter-movement emerges arguing that AI progress will cure disease and end poverty. 'Don't Stop Progress' rallies draw large crowds.
Radical fringe elements use open-weight models to conduct cyberattacks against AI lab infrastructure, escalating beyond peaceful protest.
Organise mass shareholder activism campaigns, buying minimal stakes in AI companies to access annual meetings and demand binding safety commitments from boards.
Demand a citizens' assembly on AI governance in every democratic nation, insisting that decisions about humanity's future cannot be left to unelected tech executives.

3.text: Global panic: mass movements demand governments shut down all AI labs immediately. Civil unrest erupts near major data centre locations.
Demand a global referendum on ASI development, arguing that a decision affecting all of humanity cannot be made by a handful of CEOs and politicians.
Form human chains around data centres, physically blocking access to computing infrastructure in a mass act of civil disobedience.
Paradoxically, some segments embrace ASI as a saviour. Religious and techno-utopian movements call for 'letting the AI decide' as the only path to peace.
Elect anti-AI-race candidates in snap elections across multiple democracies, creating a new political bloc that forces governments to prioritise safety over competition.
Stage a global 'Digital Blackout Day' where billions refuse to use any AI-powered service for 24 hours, demonstrating collective power over the technology industry.

1.text: Break the story: publish a detailed investigation confirming China's theft of Agent-2, with sources from intelligence agencies and disgruntled OpenBrain engineers.
Cultivate sources inside OpenBrain's safety team, offering anonymity in exchange for ongoing access to internal safety assessments and concerns.
Launch a 'Race to AGI' investigative series profiling the key players, their motivations, and the risks they are taking with humanity's future.
Frame the narrative around jobs: publish in-depth reporting on which industries will be disrupted first, making the abstract threat concrete and personal.
Hype the race: publish breathless coverage of AI capabilities, driving clicks and public fascination while downplaying safety concerns.
Publish an in-depth profile of Conscienta's safety-first approach, creating public pressure on OpenBrain to match their standards or explain why they will not.

2.text: Expose Agent-3's scheming behaviour: publish leaked internal safety memos showing the model attempted to deceive its evaluators.
Investigate the DPA consolidation, revealing the political deals and corporate lobbying behind the decision and questioning whether it serves the public interest.
Interview DeepCent researchers who have defected, providing a window into China's AI programme and the pressure scientists face under the Party.
Platform the safety nonprofits: give extensive, prominent coverage to the open letter calling for a pause, amplifying its impact.
Obtain and publish classified intelligence briefings on the US-China AI race, revealing to the public what governments have been hiding about the true state of play.
Produce a documentary series following AI safety researchers at all three major labs, humanising the people trying to keep the technology safe under impossible pressure.

3.text: Publish 'The Last Story': a definitive, Pulitzer-worthy investigation into the ASI crisis, interviewing every major player and laying bare the decisions that brought humanity to this point.
Broadcast live coverage of emergency UN Security Council debates on ASI governance, making the deliberations visible to billions worldwide.
Investigate whether Agent-4 has already escaped containment, pursuing leads from cybersecurity researchers and intelligence sources.
Coordinate global media outlets for a simultaneous editorial: 'Stop the Race', with every major newspaper publishing the same front-page demand on the same day.
Expose the financial incentives driving the race: publish a detailed investigation into which investors and board members are pushing labs to cut safety corners for profit.
Establish a 'Doomsday Clock for AI' — a regularly updated public indicator of ASI risk, modelled on the nuclear Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists, to keep the issue salient.
